topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #17 Tier 2 (RB7, TEN) and RSP #14 DoT 84.1 — both boards confirm late-Tier-2 value, and at 1.16 he's the cleanest Tier-2 RB likely to slip given the WR-heavy run ahead. Roster is RB-thin (only 4) versus 7 WRs and 5 TEs, so positional need aligns with board value.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #22 Tier 3 looks like a reach at 1.16, but RSP has him #4 overall (DoT 87.2, Nico Collins comp) — medium-RSP weighting (25%) pulls him up enough to consider if Singleton is gone. NYG landing spot is tepid, which is why Consensus dropped him; Waldman thinks the talent overrides it.

topTargets.name: Chris Bell
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #19 Tier 2 (WR10, MIA) and RSP #16 DoT 83.9 with an A.J. Brown high-end comp. Last surviving Tier-2 name at 1.16 in many scenarios; medium-RSP GM is comfortable taking him here because both boards corroborate.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Round 3 target at 3.11 — Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WR14, WAS), RSP #18 DoT 83.9 with a Jayden Reed comp Waldman likes, ADP #55 (29.06) confirms market discount. Classic medium-RSP value play in the 30s-40s where my 25/25 blend rewards confluence.

topTargets.name: Bryce Lance
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #38 (WR23, NO) but RSP #22 DoT 83.5 with an A.J. Green high-end comp — exactly the kind of late-round lottery ticket a medium-RSP GM weights up at 3.11 when Consensus has him as a free-agent-tier flier. NO offense is a real opportunity.

capPosture: Healthy: $33.9M used of $45M ($11.1M space) with 0 taxi slots filled — can active-roster either rookie or stash on taxi at 50% to bank flexibility for in-season moves.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Already 5 TEs rostered so the position is saturated, but Consensus #21 Tier 3 (TE2, PHI) and RSP #11 DoT 84.4 with a classic move-TE profile is hard to ignore at 1.16 if both Singleton and Fields are gone. Medium-RSP blend rewards the dual-board confluence even against positional logic — would mean a TE cull elsewhere.

summary: JOCKS at 1.16 is hunting the best Tier-2 survivor with RB lean, given a 4-RB room behind 7 WRs and 5 TEs. Medium RSP affinity keeps me anchored to Consensus tiers but willing to accept Fields one tier early because RSP #4 + 25% weight makes him look underpriced. At 3.11 I'm chasing RSP/Consensus confluence sleepers (Antonio Williams, Bryce Lance) rather than ADP-only names — taxi is wide open, so the round 3 pick can be a pure development bet.
